LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF REX CLACK'S AMENDED MOTIONS AND JOINT MOTION TO QUASH AND PROTECTIVE ORDER, THE RECOMMENDATION OF THE DISCOVERY PRO TEM JUDGE IS ADOPTED IN FULL. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO QUASH DEPOSITION SUBPOENA AND FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER AND SANCTIONS IS GRANTED IN PART. SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR DETAILS. Atty. CHRISTINA KIM, ESQ. of Gilsleider, McMahon & Molinello, Tel No. 925-446-3115 appearing for the Defendant Crystal Renee Moberg.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court modifies its tentative ruling, and orders as follows: As to PLAINTIFF REX CLACK'S Amended Motions And Joint Motion To Quash And Protective Order, the recommendation of the discovery pro tem judge is adopted in full. That recommendation is: Plaintiff's motion to quash deposition subpoena and for a protective order and sanctions is granted in part. Defendant may not take the deposition of attorney David Russo. The deposition of opposing counsel may be taken only under extraordinary circumstances where that attorney has information or knowledge unobtainable from other witnesses and crucial to the litigation. (Hickman v. Taylor, 329 U.S. 495, 513, 516-517; Carehouse Convalescent Hospital v. Superior Court (2006)143 Cal.App.4th 1558 and case law cited therein). However, there is a wage loss claim in this matter, and defendant does have a right to probe plaintiff's asserted wage loss. Defendant may serve a new subpoena on plaintiff's law firm, Sterling, Clack & Russo, for the person most knowledgeable regarding billing practices and Mr. Clack's wage loss. The firm shall designate and produce for deposition a competent witness as required by C.C.P. 2025.230. Sanctions are denied. The notice did not identify person against whom sanctions were sought. See CCP 2030.040. Also, no sanctions amount was stated in the declaration. The new subpoena and deposition will be limited to Mr. Clack's billing one year before and one year after the accident subject to a protective order where the information will not be used for any purpose other than this case. Defense counsel shall prepare and submit a proposed order to the Court for signature in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Nancy E. Presant-McDonald, CSR 906, Tel No. 818-551-7300/800-288-3376 (302/HK)
